intro: Your <1>datasets</1> are the focus. Each dataset has a "<3>schema (vocabulary)</3>" and "<5>ingest rules</5>".

focusBanner.label: Vocabulary class for the Ask citation:
focusBanner.sub: This answer is based on data typed with this class.

empty.title: No datasets yet
empty.sub: Ingest data via "Add data" and it will appear here.

sharedBand.title: Shared terms
sharedBand.warn: Changes affect everything · handle with care
sharedBand.sub: The schema multiple datasets share. Keeping it aligned lets you <1>search and compare across datasets</1>.

crosswalkBand.title: Connections
crosswalkBand.sub: Connect datasets that share the same concept (composition, crystal system, author…) with <1>a single bridge</1>. It can answer "how many datasets report this value?" across datasets.

tools.none: This dataset has no answerable tools yet because it has not been imported. Run "Import data" first.

files.head: Ingested files
files.dedupe: Every file that makes up this dataset. Files with the same name or contents are detected automatically at ingest time, so you never wonder "did I add this already?".
files.futureDedup: * Content-hash duplicate detection is coming; for now you can check by file name.
files.none: No ingested-file records yet. Use the operations below to ingest.

connect.head: This dataset's connections
connect.none: This dataset is not yet connected to other data.

mapBand.title: Overview
mapBand.sub: See <1>what data exists and how it connects</1> in a single map.

design.title: Schema (structure of the content)
design.predicatesHead: Predicates in use (vocabulary of the actual data)
design.reusesHead: Vocabulary borrowed from elsewhere (detected from the actual data namespaces)

ingest.head: Import data
ingest.noRml: This design has no declarative RML mapping, so it cannot be ingested. Generate §RML (declarative mapping) via "AI design" in the workbench, and you will be able to load it safely from here.
ingest.done: ✓ Ingested into the draft graph ({{n}} triples). Next, press "Publish for search" below so Ask can cite it.
ingest.note: On approval, Morph-KGC runs this declarative RML (no generated code runs — only vetted Tier 0 functions) and loads the result into an <1>isolated draft graph</1>. The Ask citation surface (canonical) is left untouched.
ingest.sourceSaved: The design-time {{source}} is saved{{files}}. Ingest with no re-attach.
ingest.pickPlaceholder: Choose the {{source}} used for the design
ingest.validationHead: Validating the RML mapping before ingest found these problems (fix them and try again):

append.head: Append to live (device feed)
append.note: Ingest only the new batch {{source}} and append it to the shared data (live) as-is (O(new)). Existing facts/IRIs are unchanged and identical rows are deduplicated. The schema and first version were approved at publish time, so appends do not go through the gate (for continuous device logs). Match the filename to the RML rml:source.
append.done: ✓ Appended (+{{n}} triples · batch #{{seq}}). Ask can cite it immediately.

promote.ok: ✓ Published for search ({{n}} triples{{version}}). Ask can cite it (canonical graph).
promote.repromoteNote: A new version is ingested into the draft graph. Pressing "Update publication (re-publish)" bumps the version Ask cites v{{version}} → v{{next}} and switches to the new data. The switch is instant and the old version is cleaned up automatically (the current version keeps being cited during ingest, so there is no gap).
promote.note: Publishing for search moves this data in the draft graph into the <1>canonical graph that Ask cites</1>. Before publishing, you can check whether the vocabulary in use is reused or new.
promote.alignmentSummary: Predicates: reuse {{predReuse}} / new {{predNew}} ／ Classes: reuse {{classReuse}} / new {{classNew}}
promote.submit: Publish for search (make usable in Ask)

reingest.head: Update data (re-ingest)
reingest.notePrefix: Ingest the new {{source}} into a separate version graph (canonical/{{id}}/v…).
reingest.notePublished: The current published version (v{{version}}) keeps being cited by Ask during ingest, so answers are not interrupted. When done, "Update publication (re-publish)" below switches v{{version}} → v{{next}} and the old version is cleaned up automatically.
reingest.noteUnpublished: Re-ingests an unpublished draft (no effect on Ask). When done, you can publish it via "Publish for search" below.
reingest.sourceSaved: The design-time {{source}} is saved{{files}}. Ingest with no re-attach. To swap in a different {{source}}, select it below.
reingest.placeholderKeep: If you do not replace it, the saved {{source}} is used

redesign.head: Revise the design
redesign.note: Reopen this schema and ingest rules in the workbench to fix or adjust them, then save (it updates the same dataset — no need to delete and recreate). After saving, apply the data via "Update data (re-ingest)".
redesign.noProposal: This dataset has no stored design to reopen in the workbench (it was created before the design was persisted). Please recreate it instead.

lifecycle.head: Lifecycle actions
lifecycle.retractedStatus: Status: <1>Retracted</1> (excluded from Ask citations. Data and IRIs remain, so existing citations are not broken. You can reinstate it.)
lifecycle.retractConfirm:
Retract this dataset?
It will be removed from Ask citations, but the data and IRIs remain, so existing citations are not broken (you can reinstate it later).
lifecycle.deleteConfirmPromoted:
Permanently delete this dataset?
It is published (citable), so existing citations may return 404. "Retract" is usually recommended.

Delete anyway?
lifecycle.deleteConfirm: Delete this dataset? (Not published, so it is safe to delete.)

docAppend.head: Add a document (to this dataset)
docAppend.note: Add one more Word <0>.docx</0> / structured XML <1>.xml</1> / PDF, and it is structured into sections → paragraphs → sentences and <2>appended directly to the shared data (live)</2>. From then on, <3>search_text</3> / <4>quote_with_citation</4> in the "Tools" tab <5>search and cite the added document too</5> (for accumulating meeting minutes). <6>.docx</6> and PDF are auto-converted to XML server-side (the converter and version are recorded in provenance).
docAppend.done: ✓ Document added (+{{n}} facts). You can search and cite the full text from the "Tools" tab.
docAppend.doneN: ✓ Added {{docs}} document(s) (+{{n}} facts). You can search and cite the full text from the "Tools" tab.

api.ontologyDescription: A design materialized in the workbench ({{date}}). A draft before promotion into the shared vocabulary.

rmlSummary.promoted: Declarative RML (ingested into shared data · {{n}} triples)
rmlSummary.ingested: Declarative RML (ingested into draft graph · {{n}} triples)
rmlSummary.design: Declarative RML (not ingested. Can be ingested via the workbench human gate.)

mappingDescription.promoted: Ingested into shared data (the canonical graph Ask cites) · {{n}} triples. Queryable via SPARQL.
mappingDescription.ingested: Ingested into the draft graph · {{n}} triples. Once verified, "Publish for search" makes it an Ask citation target.
mappingDescription.design: Design only — no RDF generated yet. With declarative RML, it can be ingested via the workbench human gate.
